summary: The UI TypeScript compilation step failed: the TypeScript compiler (tsc) exited with a non-zero status during the ts-compile-lint-ui step.
root_cause: The TypeScript compiler invocation failed: 'pnpm tsc' returned a non-zero exit status (2) when run with the generated tsconfig file '/home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json'.

evidence.reason: Shows the UI compile/format/lint step failed.

evidence.quote: subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['pnpm', 'tsc', '--p', '/home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json']' returned non-zero exit status 2.
evidence.reason: Direct error from the TypeScript compiler invocation indicating tsc returned a failure exit code.
Shows the commands were being run in the UI package directory prior to the tsc invocation (context for where the compilation ran).
The hook name present in the hooks list corresponds to the TypeScript compile/lint step that failed.

suggested_fix: Run the TypeScript compiler locally to see the full errors and fix the reported TS issues. For example, from the repo root run: pnpm tsc --p /home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json (or run pnpm --filter ./src/airflow/ui tsc) to reproduce the errors, then fix the TypeScript errors shown and re-run the CI.

summary: The race-test job failed because unit tests failed (3 test failures) — a panic from a testify/mock unexpected call to RepoServerServiceClient.GenerateManifest plus data-race warnings caused the test run to exit non‑zero.
root_cause: A unit test panicked due to an unexpected/mock mismatch: the mock for RepoServerServiceClient.GenerateManifest was called more times than expected, triggering a panic and test failure; data race(s) were also detected during the run.

evidence.quote:
assert: mock: The method has been called over 1 times.
	Either do one more Mock.On("GenerateManifest").Return(...), or remove extra call.
	This call was unexpected:
		GenerateManifest(context.backgroundCtx,*apiclient.ManifestRequest)
evidence.reason: Shows the testify/mock panic: the mock was called more times than configured and explicitly suggests adding a Mock.On("GenerateManifest").Return(...) or removing the extra call.

evidence.quote: /usr/bin/tar: Exiting with failure status due to previous errors
evidence.reason: Earlier step failed to restore tar cache (non-blocking to tests) — indicates a separate restore/cache issue occurred before tests started.
The race detector reported data races during test execution; running with -race (this job) will fail if races are present or can cause flaky failures.

evidence.quote: testing.go:1712: race detected during execution of test
evidence.reason: Confirms the Go race detector found at least one race, which is relevant because the job runs tests with -race.

evidence.quote:
=== [[31mFailed[[0m
    app_test.go:1936:
         Error Trace:    /home/runner/work/argo-cd/argo-cd/cmd/argocd/commands/app_test.go:1936
         Error:      Not equal:
evidence.reason: Shows an assertion failure in app_test.go at line 1936 — one of the failing tests that caused the test run to exit non-zero.

evidence.quote:
DONE 7346 tests, 5 skipped, 3 failures in 468.930s
make: *** [Makefile:473: test-race-local] Error 1
evidence.reason: Final summary: test run completed with 3 failures and make returned a non-zero exit, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failing test locally with the race detector (e.g. make test-race-local or go test ./... -race). Inspect the failing test at cmd/argocd/commands/app_test.go:1936 and the controller test around controller/appcontroller_test.go:2192. Update the mock expectations so GenerateManifest is expected (add Mock.On("GenerateManifest").Return(...)) or remove the extra call as indicated by the panic message: "Either do one more Mock.On(\"GenerateManifest\").Return(...), or remove extra call." Also investigate and fix any reported data races so tests pass reliably under -race, then re-run the test target.

summary: The test run failed because the test_tcl test suite had two failing tests; the CI reran the failed test and it still failed, causing the overall job to fail.
root_cause: The failure was caused by failing tests in test.test_tcl (TclTest) — 'test_tcl failed (2 failures)', and the re-run of the failed tests also failed.

evidence.reason: Shows that the test_tcl test suite had two failing tests.

evidence.quote: Re-running 1 failed tests in verbose mode in subprocesses
evidence.reason: Indicates the test harness attempted to re-run the failed test(s) for diagnostic output.
Shows that the re-run did not fix the failure; the test(s) remained failing.
The exact test invocation used in CI; useful for reproducing the failing test run locally.
Shows an example of a Tcl test method run in this suite (used to identify the test module and class).
Confirms this was a full test run and provides context that the failure came from the large test run (not a setup error earlier).

suggested_fix: Reproduce and debug the failing tests locally by running the same test invocation in verbose mode, e.g. run 'D:\a\cpython\cpython\PCbuild\amd64\python_d.exe -m test test.test_tcl -v' to capture full tracebacks; inspect the failing assertions/tracebacks and fix the test or the code under test. If failures relate to Tcl/Tk integration, verify the Tcl/Tk environment on the CI image.

summary: The build/configure checks reported many missing headers and undeclared builtins during Meson/CMake checks, and the test run later crashed with a Python segmentation fault.
root_cause: Meson/CMake preliminary checks and compile tests failed due to missing headers and undefined builtins (e.g. Python.h, isfinite), leading to an incorrect or broken build and subsequent runtime segmentation fault.

evidence.quote: numpy/meson.build:445: WARNING: The variable(s) 'BLAS_INCLUDEDIR', 'BLAS_LIBDIR', 'BLAS_OPENBLAS_CONFIG', 'BLAS_PCFILEDIR', 'BLAS_TYPE_NAME', 'BLAS_VERSION', 'LAPACK_INCLUDEDIR', 'LAPACK_LIBDIR', 'LAPACK_OPENBLAS_CONFIG', 'LAPACK_PCFILEDIR', 'LAPACK_TYPE_NAME', 'LAPACK_VERSION' in the input file 'numpy/__config__.py.in' are not present in the given configuration data.
evidence.reason: Meson configuration reported missing configuration variables, indicating a potentially incomplete or misconfigured build environment.
The build explicitly failed a check because the Python development header 'Python.h' was not found.

evidence.quote: /home/runner/work/numpy/numpy/build/meson-private/tmpati5_57k/testfile.c:9:32: error: 'isfinite' undeclared (first use in this function)
evidence.reason: A compile-time error shows standard math builtin 'isfinite' is undeclared, indicating missing or incompatible C headers/defines.
CMake preliminary checks failed, signaling the build configuration did not succeed.
The test run terminated with a segmentation fault, a likely symptom of a broken or miscompiled extension.

suggested_fix: Ensure the build environment has the needed development headers and toolchain (install Python development headers so 'Python.h' is available and provide standard C headers/compatibility for isfinite/isnan/signbit). Re-run Meson/CMake configuration and the build (e.g., install the system packages that provide Python.h and libc development headers, then run meson setup/configure and rebuild).

summary: An assembly test failed because FileCheck did not find expected CHECK patterns in the generated assembly for wasm_exceptions.
root_cause: FileCheck verification failed: the expected CHECK strings in /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs were not found in the generated assembly /checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s.

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed.

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:38:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: FileCheck reported that a CHECK at line 38 of the test file did not match the assembly input.
Confirms the failure mode is FileCheck verification (pattern-matching of expected output).
Shows the generated assembly around the scan point; FileCheck scanned from this location and did not find the expected pattern.
Identifies the generated assembly file that was checked.
Identifies the FileCheck input (the test with CHECK patterns) that failed to match.

suggested_fix: Compare the generated assembly file /checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s with the CHECK patterns in /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs and either update the test's CHECK lines to match the new assembly output or adjust the codegen so the assembly includes the expected patterns. Re-run the assembly test (FileCheck) locally to verify the fix.
